steps.title: Welcome to Day 13
steps.body: One of the most practical uses of AI: preparation. Meetings, calls, interviews, difficult conversations — AI can help you prepare in minutes instead of hours.

steps.title: Meeting prep prompt
steps.prompt: I have a meeting tomorrow about [topic]. Here is what I know: [context]. The attendees are: [people] with these roles: [roles]. Help me prepare: (1) What are the key points I should raise? (2) What questions should I expect? (3) What should I follow up on after?

steps.title: Prep check
steps.question: What is the biggest benefit of using AI for meeting prep?
options: AI attends the meeting for you
You walk in organized instead of scrambling
feedback: AI cannot attend for you — but it can make you much more effective.
Exactly. Preparation separates a good meeting from a wasted one.
AI can help organize, but your calendar is yours.
AI can help you stay focused, which can make meetings more efficient.

steps.title: A difficult conversation
steps.body: You need to give feedback to a team member who has been underperforming. Without AI: you stress, rehearse in your head, and either over-prepare or avoid it. With AI: role-play the conversation, get feedback on your approach, and walk in confident.

steps.title: Role-play prompt
steps.prompt: I need to have a difficult conversation with my [colleague/friend/family member] about [issue]. Act as that person. I will say what I have prepared. After I say it, respond as they would. Then give me feedback on how I handled it and how I could improve.

steps.title: Preparation checklist
steps.body: For any important conversation: (1) Ask AI to predict what the other person might want or need. (2) Ask AI to draft a few opening lines. (3) Role-play the conversation with AI as the other person. (4) Ask for post-meeting follow-up templates.

steps.title: Prepare for your next meeting

fields.label: What type of meeting or conversation?
What makes this conversation important?
fields.placeholder: I want to ask for a promotion

fields.label: What is the best possible outcome?
fields.placeholder: agreement on a plan for the next 6 months

steps.template: I have a {{meeting_type}} coming up. The stakes: {{stakes}}. The ideal outcome: {{outcome}}. Prepare me: what should I say, what should I expect them to say, and what should I follow up on?

steps.title: Save your prep notes
steps.body: Paste the preparation AI gave you. Review it before your meeting.

steps.title: Interview prep
steps.body: Interviews are the ultimate prep test. Ask AI: 'I am interviewing for a [role] at [company]. Give me 10 likely questions based on my resume [paste resume]. Then help me script strong answers using the STAR method.'

steps.title: Spot the good prep tasks
steps.body: Which of these are good uses of AI for preparation? Scripting a sales call, deciding what to wear to a party, preparing for a performance review, predicting what your client will ask, choosing a restaurant for dinner.
highlights: predicting what your client will ask

steps.title: True or false?
steps.question: You should only use AI for formal business meetings, not for personal conversations.
feedback: AI works for any important conversation — personal or professional.
Right. Whether it is a difficult chat with a friend or a client meeting, prep helps.

questions.label: What conversation or meeting would AI help you prepare for this week?
How much more confident would you feel walking in prepared?

steps.title: Good for AI prep?
steps.question: Role-play a negotiation with a potential client so I can practice my responses.
feedback: Yes. Role-playing with AI is excellent practice.
Exactly. Practice makes perfect, and AI is a patient practice partner.

steps.title: Good for AI prep?
steps.question: Read the other person's mind and tell me exactly what they are thinking.
feedback: AI cannot read minds. It can predict based on context, but not know for certain.
Right. AI predicts likely responses, it does not read minds.

questions.question: What is one way AI can help with difficult conversations?
options: It has the conversation for you
Role-play the other person so you can practice
It sends an apology email automatically

questions.question: AI can help prepare for both professional meetings and personal conversations.
Practicing a conversation with AI playing the other person is called ______.

steps.title: Day 13 complete — Preparation Pro
steps.body: You can now use AI to prepare for any important conversation. Tomorrow: your final day of Week 2 — building your personal AI system.
